themes: One of the primary themes in "Exodus 2023" is the collective journey of the self. The fact that all 31 figures represent the same protagonist dressed differently highlights the idea that an individual's life is made up of multiple versions of the self—different phases
personas
or roles that one assumes over time. This visual repetition suggests that
despite the outward differences
there is an underlying unity in the personal journey. The image speaks to the idea of internal multiplicity
where one person contains multitudes
each version of the self marching toward the same destination. Another key theme is migration and displacement. The title "Exodus 2023" evokes a sense of mass movement or forced migration

alluding to the biblical exodus where people flee from hardship toward an uncertain future. The figures moving in the same direction suggest a collective search for something better or a shared desire to escape the past. However
in this case
the exodus is not of many people

but of the same person in different iterations—implying that the journey is both deeply personal and universally human. The sense of anonymity and uniformity among the figures further emphasizes the theme of identity and individuality. Although each figure is dressed differently

their hidden faces suggest that identity becomes blurred in the larger context of the journey. The image asks the viewer to consider how much of individuality is retained or lost in the face of overwhelming challenges or long-term transitions. The repeated figure could also suggest a struggle with identity or a search for the true self amid the many roles one plays throughout life. Finally
the foggy and distant horizon introduces the theme of uncertainty and the unknown. The mist obscuring the destination suggests that the future is unclear
and the journey is fraught with ambiguity. The figures walking toward this unclear endpoint represent the universal human experience of moving through life without knowing what lies ahead
underscoring the existential uncertainty that defines much of the human condition.
images.teaser: Which versions of ourselves keep marching when the destination remains hidden from view?

images.name: Fake Memories
images.description: The image titled "Fake Memories" presents a surreal and emotionally charged scene. A man, presumably an older version of the protagonist, sits alone on a swing in an empty playground. The swing beside him is vacant, yet shadows on the ground in the foreground reveal the outlines of two figures holding hands—a child and an adult. This creates a haunting juxtaposition between the physical absence of companions and the lingering presence of these figures in memory. The image is toned in sepia, evoking nostalgia and the passage of time, with a textured overlay suggesting the distortion of old memories. The leaves blowing in the wind further enhance the mood of melancholy and loss.
themes: The themes of "Fake Memories" explore the unreliability of memory
aging
and the passage of time. The image evokes feelings of loneliness and nostalgia
with the swing as a metaphor for childhood and the fleeting nature of human connections. The shadows of the child and adult
though not physically present
symbolize memories that linger
yet are intangible—perhaps representing lost loved ones or past experiences. The idea of "fake" memories touches on the malleability of recollection
how time alters our perception of past events
and the possibility of romanticizing or fabricating certain elements of our history. The sepia tones and dreamlike quality of the image emphasize the distance between the present and the past
reinforcing the surrealist approach to exploring themes of personal history and emotional reflection.
images.teaser: What memories sustain us when loneliness begins inventing companions from the past?

images.name: Fluid Time
images.description: In the image titled "Fluid Time," the protagonist stands in an urban street at night, glancing down at his wristwatch with a look of concern or frustration. The setting is a dimly lit cityscape, with warm streetlights casting a soft glow over the cobblestone street. A large, vintage clock tower dominates the background, its face illuminated against the twilight. The urban environment is rendered in shades of orange and gold, enhancing the nostalgic atmosphere of the scene.
themes: The primary theme in "Fluid Time" is the fluidity and subjectivity of time
illustrated through the presence of the warped
melting clocks. These clocks suggest that time is not a rigid
unchanging force but rather something that bends and warps depending on perception. The protagonist’s focus on his wristwatch
juxtaposed against the surreal clocks floating around him
highlights the tension between humanity's attempt to measure and control time versus time’s natural
fluid nature. The image evokes feelings of frustration or anxiety related to the passage of time
a theme that may reflect personal concerns about aging
mortality
or the fleeting nature of life. Another theme is the contrast between time as experienced and time as measured. The clock tower in the background
standing tall and unmoving
represents structured time—the traditional
societal construct that governs daily life. In contrast
the melting clocks symbolize personal time
which can stretch
compress
or distort depending on circumstances
emotions
or memories. This theme touches on the existential idea that while society demands strict adherence to time
personal experience of time is far more fluid and subjective. The figure walking away in the background adds a layer of introspection
suggesting that some individuals are unaware or indifferent to the passage of time. In contrast
the protagonist’s preoccupation with his wristwatch implies a sense of urgency or anxiety about time running out

reinforcing themes of personal reflection on the unstoppable march of time and the impact it has on one's life. The cityscape setting introduces the theme of modernity and the pressure that contemporary life places on the individual to keep up with time. The urban environment
with its glowing lights and towering clock
symbolizes the societal structures that enforce strict timekeeping
deadlines
and schedules. Against this backdrop
the melting clocks serve as a counterpoint
symbolizing the personal
emotional side of time that often defies these rigid structures. In "Fluid Time
" the clash between the personal and societal views of time is palpable
creating a visual narrative that explores both the anxiety and the surreal nature of how time is experienced.
images.teaser: Why do we chase control when time keeps dissolving in our hands?

images.name: Fractaled Identity
images.description: In "Ablution," five versions of the artist inhabit a private, intimate bathroom scene, each performing a different act of routine self-care. One version of the artist is seated on the toilet, absorbed in a smartphone, another is brushing teeth at the sink, and a third is in the bathtub, submerged in bubbles. A fourth figure stands with their back turned, facing away, while the fifth is seen as a reflection in the mirror, amplifying the fragmented sense of identity and time. This depiction captures a personal moment where different facets of the self converge within the same space, engaging in mundane tasks.
themes: The addition of the fifth version—the reflection—reinforces the idea of fractured identity
memory
and time
subtly blurring the line between the real and the reflected. The repeated figure
each engaged in routine but isolated tasks
becomes a symbol of self-examination and the passage of time. The bathroom setting enhances the theme of vulnerability and intimacy
connecting self-care rituals with deeper existential questions. As the figures coexist in different temporal moments of the same ritual
they collectively express the artist's introspection on aging
daily life
and the multiplicity of self that accompanies the progression of time.
images.teaser: Which self is real when daily rituals fracture us into reflected fragments?

images.name: Fragments of Memories
images.description: The image Fragments of Memories features the protagonist, portrayed by you, standing in a river landscape surrounded by dense trees and mountains in the background. The scene is misty, evoking a sense of timelessness. In the air and water, fragmented reflections of faces appear, seemingly caught in triangular glass-like shards floating around the figure. These faces might symbolize moments or memories, evoking a sense of recollection, nostalgia, and perhaps loss. The natural surroundings create a contemplative atmosphere, contrasting with the manmade element of the floating fragments.
themes: The themes in Fragments of Memories include memory and time
as the fragments of faces suggest the scattering of memories over time. The reflections convey how memories fade
break apart
or remain only in pieces as we age
connecting this theme with the broader concept of time. The image also touches on aging and reflection
with the protagonist standing at the river
which often symbolizes the flow of time
while the reflection of faces implies a look back on life
perhaps towards significant moments or relationships. The solitude of the figure may represent the isolation one feels when reflecting on the past. Surrealism is another theme
as the combination of natural landscapes and fragmented
floating faces creates a surreal atmosphere. The floating shards defy the laws of reality
giving the impression that memories are tangible objects interacting with the physical environment. Lastly
the image evokes nostalgia and loss

with the fragmented nature of the reflections suggesting incomplete memories or the fading of recollection. This may also reflect the protagonist's contemplation of what has been lost or forgotten with time.
images.teaser: What remains of memory when the past survives only in scattered pieces?

images.name: From Answers to Questions
images.description: "From Answers to Questions" explores the existential transition from certainty to doubt. The fallen exclamation mark represents discarded beliefs or past convictions that no longer hold weight, while the floating question marks symbolize the expanding scope of inquiry and uncertainty. The protagonist's movement suggests an active pursuit of understanding, embracing the unknown rather than fearing it. The vast, desolate landscape reinforces themes of solitude and introspection, highlighting the journey of self-discovery. This surreal composition reflects the human experience of questioning, learning, and evolving beyond rigid truths.
themes: A lone figure strides down a barren desert road
his raised arm reaching toward a massive floating question mark. Behind him
smaller question marks drift in the sky
symbols of growing uncertainty. On the ground
a shattered exclamation mark lies abandoned
a remnant of lost certainty. As dust swirls around their steps
the image captures the tension between past convictions and an unfolding journey into the unknown.
images.teaser: What do we become when certainty collapses and questioning becomes our only compass?

images.name: Frozen
images.description: This image explores the themes of emotional paralysis, the passage of time, and the slow erosion of identity. The frozen figure within the ice suggests a man trapped by forces beyond his control—perhaps regret, aging, or the weight of unspoken fears. The melting edges hint at impermanence, a thawing of emotional repression or a quiet descent toward inevitability. By placing the self in a transparent prison that is both solid and melting, the image reflects the paradox of being seen yet unreachable, alive yet immobilized—a powerful metaphor for internal stasis amidst external change.
themes: In this visually striking image
a man is suspended mid-air
encased within a massive translucent cube of ice. His arms are raised as if in a gesture of surrender or alarm
and he appears frozen in motion—literally and metaphorically. The cube stands in a cold
mist-filled environment
beginning to melt and pool onto the reflective floor below. The surrounding atmosphere is dim
industrial
and impersonal
intensifying the starkness of the man’s entrapment and the eerie stillness of the scene.
images.teaser: What part of us freezes when fear turns life into visible confinement?

images.name: Frozen Echoes
images.description: Frozen Echoes is a meditation on memory, mortality, and inner reckoning. The surrounding stone figures act as fossilized echoes of the protagonist's former selves—silent witnesses to his life, choices, and transformations. Their circular arrangement suggests a symbolic tribunal or final gathering of the self, where life confronts its own sedimented history. The image invites viewers to consider the weight of time not as something abstract, but as a presence sculpted into the soul
themes: Frozen Echoes presents a living man seated in solemn stillness
eyes closed
wrapped in a white shroud. Surrounding him are eight stone-like effigies of himself
leaning inward in silent witness. Their fossilized faces echo the passage of time—identities once lived
now frozen. The dark
curtained room and sealed window add a sense of ritual and enclosure
heightening the contrast between the breathing present and the petrified past
images.teaser: How many former selves gather within us when silence becomes a final witness?

images.name: Genesis 1_26
images.description: In Genesis 1.26, the artist reimagines the biblical act of creation as a deeply personal and introspective moment, merging the divine with the self-reflective. Drawing visual inspiration from Michelangelo’s Creation of Adam, the image stages a surreal encounter between an older, godlike version of the protagonist and his younger mirrored self, blurring the lines between creator and creation, past and present, spiritual and physical. The ornate mirror becomes a threshold between dimensions, echoing the Genesis verse, “Let us make man in our image,” not as a divine decree but as a meditation on self-authorship and identity. The presence of cherubs—some classical, others mechanical—adds layers of symbolic contrast between innocence and artificiality, suggesting a tension between tradition and technological transformation. Bathed in an unnatural green hue, the entire scene evokes a dreamlike liminality where time, memory, and myth converge, inviting the viewer to ponder who we truly are: divine reflections, fragmented selves, or something entirely new.
themes: Set within a surreal
monochromatic green interior
a baroque mirror stands vertically against a richly paneled wall. Within the mirror
a man dressed in black—mid-gesture—extends his right arm and index finger upward
as if reaching or responding. Outside the mirror
a second older figure—visibly aged with a long white beard and pulled-back hair—leans in from the top right corner of the composition
his own hand extended toward the mirrored figure
their fingers almost touching. The gesture is a direct visual reference to Michelangelo’s Creation of Adam. Floating around the two central figures are three cherubs
each unique—some classical
some altered or mechanical. One cherub sits at the base of the mirror
grounding the ethereal tone. The entire image glows with an unnatural green hue
creating a dreamlike
otherworldly effect.
images.teaser: Who creates us when the mirror reflects both maker and made?

images.name: Greetings from the Dark Side of the Moon
images.description: The image, titled "Greetings from the Dark Side of the Moon," depicts a surreal scene on the lunar surface, bathed in a deep blue hue. A lone figure stands with their back to the viewer, holding an umbrella as scattered papers float around them in defiance of the Moon’s gravity. The textured surface, marked by craters and shadows, conveys the barren and desolate nature of the lunar environment. In the background, Earth looms large and vibrant, surrounded by a star-studded galaxy. The distant swirl of a galaxy adds to the cosmic expanse, emphasizing the isolation of the scene. The juxtaposition of the mundane—an umbrella and floating papers—with the vastness of space creates a strikingly surreal atmosphere.
themes: "Greetings from the Dark Side of the Moon" explores themes of solitude
displacement
and the intersection of the ordinary with the extraordinary. The figure
seemingly out of place on the Moon’s surface
symbolizes humanity’s search for meaning in unfamiliar or inhospitable environments. The floating papers suggest the persistence of human concerns—knowledge
creativity
or memories—even in the vast emptiness of space. The Earth
vibrant and close yet unreachable
underscores the longing for connection and the duality of distance and familiarity. The umbrella
a tool for protection on Earth
feels absurd in the lunar context
amplifying the surreal and existential undertones. This image invites reflection on humanity’s relationship with the cosmos
our fragility
and the imaginative leaps that connect the mundane with the infinite.
images.teaser: What earthly longings persist when distance turns home into an unreachable presence?
images.filename: Greetings from the Dark Side of the Moon.jpg

images.name: Hand Painted Self Portrait
images.description: Egghead presents a striking close-up of the artist’s face, cropped at the forehead, which emphasizes the head's round, smooth shape, resembling an egg. The minimalistic composition features muted earth tones with a soft, green background that contrasts the warm hues of the artist’s skin. The head is the only focal point, with an intense, knowing gaze directed towards the viewer. The exaggerated smoothness of the bald head, combined with the focused expression, lends the image an element of surrealism and wit, while simultaneously inviting a deeper interpretation.
themes: The primary theme in Egghead revolves around the playful distortion of identity and intellect. The title itself is a colloquial term for an intellectual or someone perceived as overly cerebral
hinting at the self-deprecating humor behind the image. There is also a theme of aging
subtly woven into the details of the skin texture and facial expression
reflecting the passage of time and the wisdom that comes with it. The image can also be viewed as a commentary on self-perception
as it uses humor and minimalism to challenge conventional ideas of appearance and self-image. The surreal element of likening the head to an egg further taps into ideas of rebirth
fragility
and absurdity
creating a layered narrative beneath the simplicity of the portrait.
images.teaser: What do we reveal when we turn intellect itself into a fragile shell?

images.name: Hatching
images.description: In the image titled "Hatching," the protagonist is depicted emerging from a large egg, surrounded by other intact eggs in an egg tray. The protagonist’s expression is one of surprise or bewilderment as he grips the edges of the cracked egg, his upper body already free from the shell. The other eggs remain whole, pristine, and untouched, creating a stark contrast between the protagonist’s active emergence and the static nature of the eggs around him. The use of the egg as a vessel from which the protagonist hatches conveys a strong sense of rebirth, renewal, or transformation.
themes: The dominant theme in "Hatching" is rebirth and transformation
represented by the protagonist’s emergence from the egg. The act of hatching typically signifies the beginning of life
but in this context

it seems to suggest a later-stage renewal or a metaphorical rebirth. The protagonist’s expression of surprise or confusion may reflect the disorientation that often accompanies personal transformations
particularly later in life. The image can be interpreted as a commentary on the continuous process of growth and change that occurs even in adulthood
challenging the notion that transformation is solely associated with youth. Another significant theme is vulnerability. The protagonist is depicted naked
emerging from the egg without the protection of the shell
symbolizing a state of exposure or defenselessness. This could suggest that transformation or personal growth often requires breaking through one’s protective barriers
leaving oneself open to new experiences and challenges. The fragility of the eggshell contrasts with the resilience required to break free from it

highlighting the tension between the delicate and the strong in moments of profound change. Humor and absurdity are also present in the image. The visual of a fully grown man hatching from an egg is inherently playful and absurd

evoking a surrealist quality that invites the viewer to question the conventional associations with birth and growth. This element of humor might serve to soften the deeper existential questions the image raises about aging
renewal
and self-discovery. Additionally
the presence of the other unhatched eggs in the background suggests that the protagonist’s experience of rebirth is unique
at least for the moment. The stillness of the surrounding eggs contrasts with the protagonist’s active emergence
possibly symbolizing that transformation is a personal
individual journey that unfolds at different times for different people. Overall
"Hatching" presents a reflection on the themes of personal growth
rebirth
and the vulnerability inherent in moments of transformation. The surreal and humorous visual elements serve to amplify the deeper existential themes
making the image both thought-provoking and whimsical.
images.teaser: What self begins to emerge when fear cracks and transformation finally starts?

images.name: Head-First Jump into the Mirror World
images.description: The image, titled "Head-First Jump into the Mirror World," captures a surreal and dramatic moment where a figure dressed in a dark coat dives headfirst through a large, ornate mirror. The mirror, framed with intricate carvings, transforms into a liquid-like portal, with ripples and splashes of water marking the point of impact. The room's vintage setting, with patterned wallpaper and dark wooden paneling, is suffused in an otherworldly green glow, heightening the surreal atmosphere. The figure's posture and motion suggest urgency or liberation, as they disappear into the unknown depths of the mirror’s reflective surface.
themes: "Head-First Jump into the Mirror World" explores themes of transformation
escape
and the pursuit of the unknown. The headfirst dive into the mirror signifies a bold leap into self-discovery or an alternate reality

rejecting hesitation and embracing the unpredictable. The liquid transformation of the mirror symbolizes the fluidity of perception and the boundaries between the real and the imagined. The splashing water evokes ideas of cleansing
rebirth
or the breaking of a metaphorical barrier. The green lighting envelops the scene with an ethereal and mysterious aura
suggesting an unfamiliar yet compelling journey. This image challenges viewers to reflect on their own willingness to take risks and confront the unfamiliar to uncover deeper truths.
images.teaser: What are we seeking when we dive headfirst into an unknown self?
images.filename: Head-First Jump into the Mirror World.jpg

images.name: Headlines
images.description: “Headlines” explores the paradox of self-awareness through a surreal inversion: the conscious self, symbolized by the severed head, is no longer in control but a reader of its own story. The book as a head speaks to how identity is shaped by internalized narratives—what we know, remember, or imagine. The image blurs the lines between intellect and flesh, presence and memory, asking whether we author our lives or merely interpret them after the fact. It’s a poignant meditation on introspection, memory, and the fragility of self-knowledge.
themes: In a stark
marble-walled corner
a barefoot man sits on the ground with his knees drawn in. Surreally
his head is replaced by an open book—its pages poised like a mind laid bare. In his hands
he gently cradles his own detached head
which is tilted forward
intently reading the book-body as though searching for answers within. The surrounding shadows and minimalist space highlight the uncanny intimacy of the act: a self trying to understand itself
mind made manifest through narrative.
images.teaser: What do we learn when the mind begins reading itself as an object?

images.name: Hostage 2
images.description: In Hostage 2, the protagonist, still wrapped in a white cloth, is now depicted in a more confined and oppressive environment, with a textured wall and a barred window reinforcing the sense of imprisonment. The figure’s hands remain bound as they pull on a rope, but this time, the tighter frame and the visible setting intensify the feeling of entrapment and helplessness. The harsh lighting and deep shadows emphasize the vulnerability of the individual, who appears completely controlled and silenced by external forces. The barred window adds a cruel irony—a glimpse of the outside world that remains unreachable.
themes: Thematically
Hostage 2 deepens the exploration of imprisonment
oppression

and the dehumanization that can occur during wartime or conflict. The Gaza War series' emphasis on the human cost of conflict is starkly portrayed in this image through the protagonist's continued struggle
now in an even more hopeless environment. The tightly bound hands and blindfolded head suggest a loss of agency
individuality
and identity. The setting—a harsh
confined space with only a small
distant view of freedom—serves as a metaphor for the internal and external prisons created by war
violence
and political strife. The rope remains a symbol of both physical and psychological bondage
reinforcing the ongoing
unresolved struggle against unseen but powerful forces.
images.teaser: How long can the self endure when confinement begins to define existence?

images.name: It's Raining Men and Clocks
images.description: In It's Raining Men and Clocks, the protagonist, represented by multiple versions of himself, is shown falling from the sky alongside an array of clocks. Both the men and the clocks are in mid-descent, defying the ordinary rules of gravity and creating an unsettling sense of chaos. The setting, a quiet, timeless street, contrasts sharply with the absurdity of this rain of men and timepieces. Some of the clocks have already shattered upon hitting the ground, while others hover in freefall, contributing to the atmosphere of impending catastrophe as both the protagonist and time itself tumble uncontrollably.
themes: Thematically
the image explores the helplessness of humans in the face of time’s unyielding progression. The clocks
a classic symbol of time
emphasize the inevitability of time slipping away
and the protagonist’s descent highlights the feeling of being caught in a freefall
unable to stop or control the passing moments. The multiplicity of the protagonist suggests a fractured sense of identity or the passage of the self through different stages of life
all subject to the same fate. The surrealism in the image evokes existential questions about the nature of time
mortality

and the human struggle to make sense of it all. The falling men and clocks together underscore a shared theme of inevitability—of life's transient nature and the inability to pause or reverse time's downward trajectory.
images.teaser: What holds us together when time itself seems to be falling apart?

images.name: In Deep Waters
images.description: The image presents a serene, dreamlike environment suffused with green tones, creating an otherworldly yet tranquil ambiance. A lone figure, dressed in dark attire, stands at the center, facing to the side, as if deep in contemplation. The figure is surrounded by tall walls draped with creeping vines and foliage, while the ground below reflects the lush greenery, suggesting a water-like surface or a polished reflective floor. In the foreground, a single yellow leaf floats delicately on the reflective surface, providing a stark contrast to the otherwise verdant and monochromatic color palette. Beyond the figure, the scene opens into a softly lit, forest-like space, evoking a sense of mystery and quiet introspection.
themes: This image explores themes of solitude
nature's dominance
and the passage of time. The reflective surface and encroaching vines symbolize the merging of the natural and constructed worlds
suggesting humanity's transient presence in an enduring natural order. The figure
solitary and still
embodies introspection
perhaps reflecting on their place within this timeless
overgrown setting. The yellow leaf serves as a poignant symbol of change and mortality
contrasting with the vibrant greenery that signifies life and renewal. The interplay of light
shadow
and reflection creates a meditative space
inviting the viewer to ponder the delicate balance between permanence and ephemerality
and the quiet moments of self-awareness that arise in isolation.
images.teaser: What inner truth appears when stillness draws us toward hidden depths?

images.name: The Waiting Room
images.description: The image titled "In the Waiting Room" presents a surreal and introspective scene featuring yourself seated in a small, dimly lit corner of a room. The space is claustrophobic, with worn, faded walls and an eerie, muted color palette. You are seated in two different positions in the same room, separated by the corner of the wall, creating a mirror-like effect between the two figures. One version of yourself is looking forward, while the other gazes off into the distance, each sitting on a yellow chair. The room's lighting and the reflections in the mirrors amplify the feeling of being caught in time, as if waiting for something indefinite. The quiet tension and the slight distortion in perspective enhance the overall surreal mood of the image.
themes: The image explores themes of waiting
time
and introspection. The waiting room setting alludes to moments of anticipation
uncertainty
and perhaps even resignation
where time seems to stretch indefinitely. The dual representation of the protagonist (yourself) symbolizes inner dialogue
self-reflection
or the fractured nature of identity and consciousness during moments of stillness. The mirrors not only create reflections but also suggest layers of perception and reality
hinting at self-awareness and the passage of time. The atmosphere of decay in the room
combined with the muted colors
evokes feelings of solitude
contemplation
and existential waiting
as if the protagonist is not only waiting physically but also emotionally or spiritually. The image subtly conveys the psychological tension of being suspended between moments
trapped in the act of waiting.
images.teaser: Who do we become while waiting for the life that refuses to arrive?

images.name: Infinity
images.description: Infinity visually portrays a sequence of recursive reflections of the artist’s face, set within a seemingly endless spiral of broken, spherical forms. Each layer of the spiral appears to reflect back on itself, creating an impression of infinite recursion. The repetitive nature of the self-reflections is emphasized by the distorted perspective, which guides the viewer's eye deep into the visual abyss, evoking the sensation of being drawn into an infinite loop. The backdrop is an abstract, cosmic swirl of deep greens and blues, suggesting a boundless, otherworldly space that aligns with the title.
themes: The dominant theme of Infinity is recursion and the cyclical nature of existence. The image explores themes of self-reflection and the concept of infinity through the endless repetition of the protagonist’s face. The visual structure evokes thoughts of the infinite
not only in terms of time but also in the existential sense of eternal self-examination. There is an underlying theme of duality between confinement and boundlessness
with the cracked spherical forms representing fragility within the infinite expanse. The image also ties into broader philosophical themes
such as the concept of the self within an endless universe and the questioning of reality versus illusion.
images.teaser: What remains of identity when the self repeats beyond any final boundary?

images.name: Inner Conflict
images.description: In the image titled "Inner Conflict," two versions of the protagonist face off in a boxing match. The figure on the left, dressed in a white shirt, is caught mid-reaction as he receives a punch to the face from the second figure on the right, who is dressed in a black shirt. Both are wearing red boxing gloves, and the intensity of the punch is evident, as the glasses of the left-hand figure have been knocked off and are captured in mid-air, just above his head. The scene is set against a plain, neutral background, allowing the focus to remain entirely on the confrontation between the two figures.
themes: The most prominent theme in "Inner Conflict" is the idea of a personal
internal struggle. The two figures
representing different aspects of the same individual
are locked in a battle
symbolizing the tension between opposing forces within the self. The use of boxing
a sport that demands both physical strength and mental focus
further emphasizes the intense nature of this internal struggle. This image can be seen as a visual metaphor for the battles we face within ourselves—whether it's between different emotions
decisions
or aspects of one's identity. The contrasting outfits—the white shirt and the black shirt—might represent the duality of the self. One side could symbolize vulnerability
passivity
or innocence
while the other could represent strength
aggression
or control. The violent act of punching could be interpreted as an attempt to suppress
confront
or resolve these conflicting elements of the self. Another key theme is the exploration of aging and self-perception. The physical act of boxing suggests a fight for control
dominance
or reconciliation between past and present selves. The image invites the viewer to consider the complexity of inner conflict
particularly as one ages
where emotional and psychological battles may take on new dimensions. This scene captures a moment of intense self-reflection
making it both a powerful visual and emotional representation of internal conflict. The simplicity of the composition allows the theme of inner struggle to come to the forefront
creating a universal narrative that viewers can relate to on a personal level.
images.teaser: Why do our fiercest battles erupt between the selves no one else sees?

images.name: Inside the Mirror World
images.description: The image depicts an elderly man standing alone in a grand, symmetrical hall lined with tall, ornate mirrors. He faces one mirror directly, his reflection staring back at him, while identical mirrors extend into the depth of the space on both sides, creating a corridor of repeated reflections. The architecture is classical and elegant—arched frames, carved details, and a chandelier suspended above—yet the entire scene is submerged in a cool, greenish haze that softens edges and blurs depth. The man is dressed in a long dark coat, his posture upright and still, positioned precisely at the center of the composition. The polished floor reflects both the figure and the mirrors, reinforcing the image’s symmetry and sense of infinite regression.
themes: The image explores self-confrontation
identity
and the recursive nature of introspection. The mirrors do not merely reflect the man; they multiply him
suggesting that the self is not singular but fragmented across perception
memory
and time. The symmetrical corridor evokes a mental or psychological space rather than a physical one—a hall of consciousness where past selves
present awareness
and imagined futures coexist. The green
mist-like atmosphere introduces an oneiric
almost submerged quality
implying introspection that is deep
isolating
and slightly disorienting. Rather than offering clarity
the mirrors produce repetition without resolution
suggesting that self-knowledge may be an endless loop rather than a destination. The work thus meditates on aging
self-observation
and the quiet unease of looking inward and finding not a definitive answer
but an infinite echo of oneself.
images.teaser: Which self confronts us when reflection multiplies what we thought was singular?

images.name: Internal Clock
images.description: The image titled Internal Clock portrays the artist as the protagonist, with his upper body seemingly transforming into a clock. The clock is encased within his chest, symbolizing a deep connection to time and its passage. The fiery, colorful backdrop adds a sense of urgency or emotional intensity, enhancing the metaphorical representation of time as a force that affects both the mind and body. The crackling energy surrounding the figure conveys a sense of disruption or fragmentation, reinforcing the central theme of time's inevitable impact on the individual.
themes: The overarching theme of time is central to this image
specifically represented through the idea of an "internal clock
" which may refer to the biological clock or the existential sense of time running out. Aging is subtly referenced through the image of the clock embedded within the protagonist’s chest
indicating the inevitable progression of time and the personal awareness of it. The surrealist elements in the composition
including the fiery background and the merging of human and object
reflect the artist's use of metaphorical visual language to convey complex ideas. The emotional intensity of the background suggests inner turmoil or contemplation
adding a psychological depth to the exploration of time.
images.teaser: How does the body change when time begins speaking from within?

images.name: Invasion of the Brain Snatchers
images.description: In the surreal image titled "Invasion of the Brain Snatchers," the protagonist stands by a waterfront, set against a backdrop of towering skyscrapers. The modern cityscape, with its futuristic, gleaming structures rising toward a bright blue sky filled with fluffy clouds, conveys a sense of urban grandeur and progress. However, the scene takes an unexpected and unsettling turn as the protagonist, dressed formally in a suit, has the head of a large fish instead of a human face. The fish-headed figure holds in his hands a glowing, transparent container within which is the head of the protagonist—his human head—staring back, seemingly detached from its body.
themes: The central theme in "Invasion of the Brain Snatchers" is the alienation of identity. The image of the protagonist holding his own head in a glowing container
while his body is now topped with a fish head
suggests that his sense of self—symbolized by the human head—has been taken
captured
or replaced. The fish-headed figure might represent external forces or influences that have "invaded" the protagonist's mind or self
leaving him detached from his own identity. The glowing container implies that the brain or the mind is being observed or analyzed

perhaps signifying the protagonist's loss of autonomy or his feeling of being controlled by forces beyond his understanding. Another significant theme is transformation and displacement. The fish head
standing in for the human mind
evokes a sense of the absurd
suggesting a kind of dehumanization or metamorphosis that has occurred against the protagonist’s will. The presence of the fish head in place of a human head could symbolize a regression
a move away from humanity toward something more primal or instinctual
reflecting the protagonist’s alienation from his own identity. The urban setting introduces the theme of modernity and technology
contrasting sharply with the surreal invasion or transformation taking place in the foreground. The sleek
modern skyscrapers towering in the background symbolize progress
technology
and the structured
controlled environment of the modern world. Yet
in the midst of this urban landscape
a bizarre

otherworldly event is unfolding. This juxtaposition suggests that beneath the surface of modern life lies chaos or an unseen force that threatens individual autonomy. The title "Invasion of the Brain Snatchers" directly references classic science fiction narratives about body-snatching or mind control
hinting at a fear of losing one’s autonomy to external
possibly alien
forces. This theme connects to broader anxieties about modern life
where technology
societal pressures

or unseen influences can lead to feelings of disconnection from one’s own mind or identity. The image also touches on themes of self-examination and self-alienation. The protagonist’s act of holding his own head suggests a form of reflection or contemplation
as though he is examining his own mind or sense of self from an external perspective. This separation between mind and body could symbolize a deeper introspection
where the protagonist is forced to confront his own transformation and the forces that have altered his identity.
images.teaser: What is stolen first when identity no longer feels entirely human?

images.name: Venetian Memories
images.description: "Venetian Memories" captures a vivid, almost fantastical representation of Venice, where not only does the water of its canals reflect the city’s iconic architecture, but the sky is alive with balloons carrying images of what appear to be historical figures or moments captured in time. The protagonist, depicted as an elder gentleman with a dignified beard and ornate attire, gazes out contemplatively, suggesting a deep connection to the floating vignettes above. The rich detail and coloration in his clothing echo the romantic and historical ambiance of Venice itself.
themes: Thematically
this image explores the concept of memory as both a personal and collective experience. The balloons symbolize the buoyancy of memories
which
like the city of Venice
are beautiful yet may float away or pop at any moment. The serene expression of the protagonist suggests a serene engagement with these floating memories
which might represent his own past or Venice’s historical legacy. The whimsical merging of the surreal with the real invites the viewer to contemplate the fragility of history and memory

encapsulated in the ethereal beauty of the floating balloons against the backdrop of a city renowned for its relationship with the past. This image serves as a metaphor for the way memories can uplift us
providing a window to look back at what has shaped us
just as Venice itself is a city steeped in history yet continually evolving.
images.teaser: Why do certain memories rise precisely when we fear losing them?

images.name: Labyrinth of the Mind
images.description: The image, titled "The Labyrinth of Thoughts," portrays a surreal and intricate composition where the profile of a human head becomes a complex architectural structure. The facial features seamlessly blend into a maze of staircases, arches, and corridors, creating a visual metaphor for the inner workings of the mind. Several small figures, all resembling the same individual, are scattered throughout the labyrinth, navigating its pathways in different directions. The use of monochromatic tones for the stone architecture contrasts with the vibrant teal shirts of the figures, drawing attention to their presence amidst the structured chaos. The details, such as the ear forming part of the labyrinth and the glasses integrated into the architecture, add layers of realism to the surreal design.
themes: "The Labyrinth of Thoughts" delves into themes of introspection
complexity
and self-discovery. The labyrinth serves as a metaphor for the intricacies of human thought
memory
and emotion
where paths are interconnected yet confusing. The repeated figures represent fragmented aspects of the self
each exploring different facets of the mind or embarking on unique journeys toward understanding. The architectural style evokes a sense of timelessness and permanence

suggesting that the labyrinth is both a personal and universal construct of human existence. The juxtaposition of the structured design with the human element reflects the tension between order and chaos within the mental and emotional landscapes. This artwork invites viewers to reflect on their own inner journeys and the perpetual search for clarity within the labyrinth of their thoughts.
images.teaser: How do we find ourselves in a mind built from endless inner corridors?

images.name: Laundry Day
images.description: In the surreal image titled "Laundry Day," the protagonist finds himself trapped inside a washing machine, peering out from behind the circular glass door. His expression is one of distress, as if screaming underwater, bubbles rising around him. The distorted face, partially submerged in water, is pressed against the glass, evoking a sense of claustrophobia and entrapment. The washing machine, with its bright white exterior and mechanical dials, is a standard household appliance, but the human presence inside it transforms this everyday object into a scene of absurdity and discomfort.
themes: One of the key themes in "Laundry Day" is entrapment within the mundane. The washing machine
an everyday object associated with domestic chores and routine

becomes a symbol of confinement. The protagonist’s distress and entrapment within this ordinary appliance may represent feelings of being overwhelmed or consumed by the repetitive cycles of modern life
where routine tasks take on oppressive qualities. The image can be seen as a metaphor for how mundane activities or societal expectations can trap individuals
leading to feelings of helplessness or frustration. Another important theme is absurdity and the surreal transformation of the familiar. By placing the protagonist inside a washing machine
the image creates an absurd scenario
turning an everyday object into something nightmarish. The absurdity of the situation adds a layer of dark humor
suggesting that even the most familiar aspects of life can become sources of existential reflection or discomfort when viewed through a surrealist lens. The water inside the machine
paired with the protagonist's distressed expression
hints at themes of drowning or suffocation
both literally and metaphorically. The washing machine’s constant
unrelenting cycle mirrors the feeling of being trapped in an endless loop
unable to break free from repetitive tasks or expectations. The protagonist’s underwater state may symbolize a sense of losing control or being submerged by external pressures
struggling to stay afloat in the relentless wash of daily responsibilities. Lastly
there’s a commentary on modernity and mechanization
where humans are increasingly at the mercy of machines and automated processes. The washing machine
a tool of convenience
is now the cause of the protagonist's distress
symbolizing how reliance on modern technology can sometimes lead to a loss of agency or personal control.
images.teaser: What part of us feels trapped inside the routines meant to cleanse us?

images.name: Le Penseur
images.description: In the image titled "Le Penseur," the protagonist is depicted in a pose reminiscent of Auguste Rodin’s famous sculpture The Thinker. The subject is nude, seated with his chin resting on his hand, deep in thought. His body is slightly slumped, and his expression reflects a sense of introspection or contemplation. The image is rendered in grayscale, emphasizing the texture of the skin and the cracked, worn surface of the wall behind him, which mirrors the aging and weathered appearance of the protagonist.
themes: The primary theme in "Le Penseur" is aging and decay. The image reflects the passage of time
as symbolized by the protagonist’s aged
deteriorating body and the cracked walls behind him. The transformation of the human form into something resembling stone or a statue hints at the inevitable decline that comes with aging
contrasting sharply with the notion of timeless
idealized beauty often associated with classical sculpture. In this way
the image speaks to the human condition
focusing on the physical and existential consequences of growing older. The second theme is contemplation and introspection
as evoked by the iconic Thinker pose. The protagonist’s deep reflection suggests that he is not only considering his own life but perhaps also the nature of existence itself. This moment of thought
combined with the visible signs of aging

introduces a meditation on mortality—considering life’s transience while being acutely aware of the body's limitations and eventual decline. The potential inclusion of this image in a series titled "Aging Statues" adds another layer of meaning. The theme of time and transformation becomes central
as the image explores how the passage of time affects both the body and the mind. In the context of this series
the protagonist may represent the gradual transformation of humans into relics of their former selves—statues that once symbolized strength
beauty
and vitality
now weathered by time and circumstance. Finally
the image touches on the theme of the human body as art. By echoing the form of The Thinker
the image places the protagonist in a lineage of classical artistic representation while simultaneously subverting it by emphasizing imperfection
age
and decay. This contrast between the idealized body and the reality of aging challenges traditional notions of beauty
urging the viewer to find meaning in impermanence and vulnerability.
images.teaser: What survives thought when age and time begin dismantling the body?

images.name: Liberation
images.description: In the image titled "Liberation," the protagonist appears in two forms once again, echoing themes from the previous work "The Puppet Master." In this piece, the upper figure, dressed in a formal suit and tie, hovers above, holding red strings that connect him to the lower figure. The lower figure, kneeling on the ground, is dressed in casual clothes and barefoot. However, unlike the previous image, the lower figure is actively cutting one of the red strings with a small knife, symbolizing an act of release or rebellion against the control imposed by the upper figure.
themes: The primary theme in "Liberation" is the act of reclaiming autonomy and the struggle for self-determination. The lower figure’s action of cutting the string is a direct metaphor for breaking free from control
manipulation
or oppressive influences. The red string
often symbolic of fate or control in mythologies
here becomes a tether that the protagonist chooses to sever
representing liberation from forces—whether psychological
societal
or internal—that have dictated his path. This theme of autonomy is contrasted by the presence of the upper figure
who remains calm
seemingly unaware of the act of rebellion below. The upper figure still holds the remaining strings
symbolizing that complete liberation may not be instantaneous or total. The ongoing presence of control suggests that the journey to full freedom is gradual
with remaining constraints still to be overcome. Another theme is personal transformation. The act of cutting the string represents a moment of decisive action
where the protagonist shifts from passive submission to active change. The knife symbolizes the tool of empowerment
and the simple act of severing a string speaks volumes about the personal power that lies in small
deliberate actions. The cracks in the floor below the lower figure suggest instability
perhaps reflecting the uncertainty or risk involved in breaking free from control. The cracks may also symbolize the breaking of old patterns or structures
making way for new forms of independence and self-direction. The contrast between the upper and lower figures represents a duality of self—the controlled and the controller. However
unlike in "The Puppet Master
" where the lower self seemed powerless
here the lower self takes steps toward emancipation
signaling hope
progress
and the possibility of self-liberation.
images.teaser: What finally changes when we cut the thread that once controlled us?

images.name: Liquid Time
images.description: In "Fluid Time 2," you, as the protagonist, are depicted in a formal suit and tie, checking your wristwatch amidst an environment where time appears distorted. The large, surreal, melting watches around you evoke a sense of time becoming fluid and ungraspable, reminiscent of Salvador Dalí’s surrealist style. The urban backdrop with glowing city lights contrasts sharply with the warped timepieces, emphasizing the clash between the rigid structure of modern life and the fluid, uncontrollable nature of time. Your expression of urgency adds a personal layer to the visual narrative, suggesting a race against the inevitable passage of time.
themes: The primary theme in this image is the fluidity and manipulation of time
symbolized by the melting clocks. This suggests that time is not rigid or fixed but can become malleable or unstable. The image also explores the tension between modernity
as seen in the sharp city backdrop
and the distortion of traditional constructs like time. There’s an underlying reflection on the pressures of time in contemporary life
especially in how the man is portrayed with a sense of urgency. Surrealism is a key artistic influence here
and the image may also nod toward existential concerns
questioning the nature of time and our control over it.
images.teaser: How do we live sanely when time itself loses its solid shape?

images.name: Me Vs King Kong
images.description: In "Me Vs. King Kong," the protagonist is depicted engaging in a direct confrontation with the legendary giant ape, King Kong. The protagonist, much smaller in scale, is shown raising their arms in a defiant posture, suggesting either a challenge or an attempt to assert dominance. King Kong, on the other hand, holds the protagonist in the palm of his enormous hand, gazing down at them with an expression that can be interpreted as either amused or curious.
themes: One of the central themes of "Me Vs. King Kong" is the confrontation between human and nature. King Kong
representing raw
untamed nature and primal strength
stands in contrast to the human protagonist
who embodies the intellectual and civilized aspects of humanity. The image may symbolize humanity's attempt to dominate or challenge the forces of nature
despite being physically outmatched. This theme touches on the age-old conflict between man and nature
where humans often overestimate their ability to control the natural world

only to be reminded of their vulnerability in its presence. Another important theme is courage and defiance in the face of insurmountable odds. The protagonist’s raised arms suggest a readiness to fight
despite being held by a creature far more powerful. This speaks to the human spirit of resilience and determination
even when facing forces much larger and more intimidating than oneself. The image may be interpreted as a commentary on personal or existential struggles
where individuals find themselves up against challenges that seem overwhelming or undefeatable
yet they continue to resist. The size difference between the two figures also alludes to power dynamics and vulnerability. The protagonist's vulnerability
both in terms of size and position
contrasts sharply with the physical dominance of King Kong. Yet
there is a sense of mutual recognition between the two
as King Kong’s expression is not overtly threatening but rather contemplative
suggesting a momentary pause in the battle between the two. Surrealism is also present in the scene

as the clash between the human and the mythical giant ape is exaggerated for dramatic effect. The image invites the viewer to suspend disbelief and enter a world where such an improbable encounter could take place
symbolizing larger-than-life battles that occur in the mind or spirit rather than the physical world.
images.teaser: What compels us to confront forces so much larger than ourselves?

images.name: Meta-Mirror-Phosis
images.description: This image explores the paradoxes of identity, time, and technological transformation, questioning the fluid boundaries between humanity and artificiality. The mirror, a classic symbol of self-reflection, presents not just an alternate appearance but a philosophical confrontation with the future—is this cybernetic form an inevitable evolution, a lost humanity, or a mere illusion? The presence of the robotic dog introduces irony, contrasting mechanical loyalty with the man’s internal existential tension. The lack of the dog's reflection suggests that some elements of life—perhaps companionship, emotion, or spontaneity—may not survive the transition into the mechanical world. By juxtaposing organic reality with a detached, synthetic self, the image invites reflection on the consequences of merging human consciousness with technology, and whether the soul can endure beyond the flesh.
themes: An elderly man stands before an ornate
dark green mirror
gazing at his own reflection—only to find a cybernetic version of himself staring back. The mirror’s glass serves as a portal to an alternate reality
where his human flesh has been replaced by metallic limbs
exposed circuits
and robotic augmentations. At his feet
a mechanical dog with glowing elements lies playfully
its tongue out
seemingly unaffected by the existential weight of the scene. The absence of the dog’s reflection adds to the unsettling surrealism
hinting that this technological transformation exists only within the mirrored world. The dimly lit
green-walled environment enhances the eerie atmosphere
blending reality with a dystopian dreamscape.
images.teaser: Which self feels more frightening when the mirror reflects our possible future?

images.name: ‏‏Moebius Loop
images.description: There's an Elephant in the Room" humorously brings the idiom to life by placing an actual elephant within the elegant setting of a refined bedroom. The protagonist, seated on the elephant and guided by a mahout, appears nonchalant despite the absurdity of the situation. The elephant’s large presence, with its wrinkled skin and adorned with fabric, contrasts sharply against the clean, polished interior of the bedroom, creating a surreal, whimsical juxtaposition. The pristine bed and chandelier enhance the sense of displacement, where the enormous animal doesn’t belong in this intimate, enclosed space.
themes: This image plays on the idiom "the elephant in the room
" which refers to an obvious problem or issue that is being ignored or unaddressed. By literalizing this metaphor
the artist uses surrealism to explore themes of denial
avoidance

and discomfort in facing difficult truths. The calm demeanor of both the protagonist and the mahout suggests a willful ignorance or an acceptance of the absurdity. The absurdity and visual humor soften the underlying commentary on how easily people overlook significant issues
perhaps within personal or societal contexts. The elephant
traditionally a symbol of memory and wisdom
becomes a focal point of contradiction—its enormous
unavoidable presence clashes with the refined normalcy of the room
pushing the viewer to question what is left unspoken or unnoticed in their own lives.
images.teaser: Why do we ignore the obvious until it begins occupying all inner space?

images.name: My Elephant Ride to Iceland
images.description: In the whimsical and surreal image titled "My Elephant Trip to Iceland," the protagonist sits atop a richly decorated elephant that wades through crystal blue waters against the backdrop of a majestic Icelandic waterfall. The waterfall cascades down the rocky green cliffs, creating mist and a sense of serene, natural beauty. Above the water, the protagonist is seated in a traditional howdah, a large red cloth covering the elephant, adding a splash of vibrant color to the otherwise natural setting. The protagonist appears to be engaging in a casual, almost playful adventure, as though taking an ordinary elephant ride—except in the unlikely and surreal setting of Iceland.
themes: One of the main themes in "My Elephant Trip to Iceland" is the fusion of opposites—the natural and the surreal
the familiar and the exotic. The unlikely combination of an elephant
typically associated with tropical or Asian landscapes
wandering through an Icelandic scene emphasizes the whimsical nature of the image. This fusion speaks to the blending of worlds or experiences that are not traditionally connected
suggesting an imaginative exploration of possibilities beyond the boundaries of reality. Another theme is adventure and exploration. The protagonist
casually riding an elephant through this surreal landscape
embodies a sense of curiosity and a willingness to explore the unexpected. The journey itself
taking place in such an unusual setting
reflects the adventurous spirit of the protagonist. The image can be seen as a metaphor for stepping out of one's comfort zone or taking a journey to uncharted
unfamiliar territories
both literally and metaphorically. The contrast between above and below the water introduces a theme of hidden depth. Above the waterline
the scene appears light and humorous
but below the surface
the presence of sharks adds an element of danger or the unknown. The juxtaposition between the peaceful
idyllic setting above and the lurking presence of sharks below may represent the idea that beneath the surface of seemingly joyful or calm experiences
there can be hidden challenges or fears. This contrast encourages viewers to consider the deeper
unseen aspects of life’s adventures. There is also an element of playfulness and absurdity in the image
where the very idea of riding an elephant through Iceland
an environment ill-suited to such a creature
evokes a sense of humor. The absurdity invites the viewer to engage with the image with a sense of imagination and open-mindedness
as if entering a dreamlike world where the rules of reality no longer apply. Finally
the title "My Elephant Trip to Iceland" adds a personal touch
suggesting that the protagonist’s adventure is not only surreal but also meaningful in a subjective or symbolic way. The scene might reflect a personal journey
one that combines unexpected elements and challenges the norms of experience.
images.teaser: What longing drives us toward impossible journeys that feel more true than reality?

images.name: Nightmare
images.description: The image Nightmare depicts a deeply unsettling scene in a dimly lit bedroom. The protagonist (you) lies on the bed, motionless, while four tall, dark, cloaked figures loom ominously in the background, casting an eerie presence over the room. Their featureless forms stand in stark contrast to the illuminated window behind them, where soft daylight penetrates the closed curtains. An old-fashioned clock hangs above the scene, its time frozen as if to suspend the moment in a state of dread. The bluish tone of the image adds to the cold, oppressive atmosphere, evoking a sense of foreboding and anxiety as though time and life itself are in a suspended state. The image is imbued with a feeling of helplessness, with the protagonist caught in a passive and vulnerable position.
themes: Nightmare explores themes of Mortality and Fear of Death
with the cloaked figures resembling Grim Reapers or death-like entities
representing the inevitable passage of time and the haunting presence of mortality. The motionless figure in the bed suggests the vulnerability of sleep
where one is exposed to unconscious fears and helplessness. The inclusion of the clock highlights the theme of Time
reinforcing the idea that time continues to tick
even in moments of fear or dread. The dark
faceless figures symbolize the unknown
alluding to existential fears and the inevitability of life's end. Additionally
there is a theme of Isolation
with the protagonist lying alone
surrounded by shadows
emphasizing a deep sense of solitude in the face of life's final moments. This image evokes an unsettling psychological landscape
where the boundaries between sleep and reality
life and death
seem to blur.
images.teaser: What terror takes shape when mortality stands silently at the edge of sleep?

images.name: Nightmares Assembly
images.description: The image depicts an elderly man lying peacefully in bed with his arms crossed over his chest, as if in eternal rest. He is surrounded by eight hooded, shadowy figures—all bearing his own likeness. These spectral doppelgängers lean in from the darkness, their faces partially illuminated, creating an unsettling circle of self-gaze. The background, a cracked and desaturated urban surface, reinforces the grim and surreal atmosphere. The only warmth lies in the pillow and bed linens, offering a stark contrast to the ghostly presence and cold tones that dominate the composition.
themes: This haunting self-portrait explores the psychological confrontation with mortality and the fragmentation of self in the face of death. The eight hooded versions of the man can be seen as embodiments of memory
regret
fear
or alternate selves—perhaps the different versions of a life lived
come to bear witness to its end. The image plays with themes of introspection
finality
and the inescapable presence of the self even at the edge of oblivion. By surrounding the living version with spectral versions of himself
the image visualizes an inner nightmare—an existential gathering at the threshold between consciousness and the void.
images.teaser: What hidden fears assemble when sleep can no longer protect the self?

images.name: On My Way to Oz
images.description: Swept up by howling winds beneath a storm-swept sky, an elderly man appears to lose his balance, arms thrown wide and glasses slipping from his hand. A floating Victorian house, its root-like underpinnings exposed, looms ominously above, echoing Dorothy’s iconic Kansas farmhouse caught in a tornado. An overturned car hovers nearby, emphasizing the magnitude of this swirling chaos and blurring the line between everyday life and surreal fantasy. The palette of dark clouds, streaks of lightning, and displaced objects conveys both urgency and wonder, suggesting a sudden rupture in the natural order.
themes: Drawing on the disorienting power of classic Wizard of Oz imagery
the scene captures a pivotal moment of transformation—when reality is quite literally torn from its foundation. Just as Dorothy’s tornado journey leads her to self-discovery
the man’s wrenching flight into the unknown symbolizes the universal human experience of being uprooted by forces beyond control. The exposed roots of the floating house
the overturned car
and the tumbling glasses represent the fragility of our familiar constructs
urging reflection on what truly anchors us. At once haunting and exhilarating
the tableau underscores how unexpected upheavals can become catalysts for profound insight.
images.teaser: What longing drives us toward imagined worlds when reality becomes unbearable?

images.name: On the Couch
images.description: The scene unfolds in a warmly lit, domestic room, centered around a psychological therapy session. An older man lies on a white couch in the classic pose of a patient, while another version of the same man — the therapist — sits across from him with notepad in hand. Hovering above the reclining figure is a large, translucent bubble, filled with chaotic, surreal imagery: a screaming face, a mushroom cloud, ghostly hands, and a crumbling building. Smaller bubbles float around it like fragments of disturbed memory. The bubble’s surface and its internal content suggest both fragility and volatility. The room is modern and calm, in stark contrast to the inner world projected above the man’s head.
themes: “On the Couch” delves into the fractured psyche
exploring inner trauma
memory distortion
and emotional repression. The mirrored self—therapist and patient—implies deep introspection or self-analysis
as if the protagonist is both the source of pain and the seeker of understanding. The dreamlike
symbolic content within the bubble represents surfacing subconscious material
especially elements of fear
war
existential dread
and suppressed anguish. The image resonates with themes of mental health
identity fragmentation
and the blurred boundary between internal and external reality.
images.teaser: Can we heal what hurts us when therapist and patient share one mind?

images.name: Orange Juice
images.description: In the image titled "Orange Juice," the familiar and mundane act of preparing fresh juice takes on a surreal twist. The central composition features a glass of freshly squeezed orange juice, surrounded by whole and sliced oranges. A manual orange juicer sits to the left, its vibrant orange color mirroring the fruit on display. The backdrop, a kitchen wall adorned with the image of an oversized autumn leaf, adds a subtle yet symbolic touch to the scene.
themes: The bubble becomes a metaphor for psychic containment — a delicate enclosure where trauma is observed but not yet burst or resolved. This work fits powerfully within your Alone with Myself series and can also overlap with the Paradoxes of Existence theme due to its self-reflective duality and psychological complexity.
images.teaser: What part of us seeks safety inside fragile rituals of containment and observation?

images.name: Ozymandias
images.description: In the image titled "Ozymandias," the scene is set in a vast desert landscape at sunset, bathed in warm, reddish tones. The viewer is immediately reminded of Percy Bysshe Shelley’s poem, as colossal, decayed statues and fragments of stone, resembling your likeness, are scattered across the barren landscape. Two enormous, weathered stone legs stand on a crumbled pedestal in the center of the image, towering over the surrounding ruins. These cracked and broken legs are all that remain of a once grand statue, evoking a sense of lost grandeur and decay.
themes: The central theme of "Ozymandias" is the impermanence of human power and legacy

a direct reference to Shelley's famous poem. The shattered remnants of the once-grand statue—represented by your likeness—serve as a powerful reminder of the futility of human ambition in the face of time. The colossal legs
now standing alone in the desert
symbolize how even the mightiest of rulers or achievements eventually crumble to dust. The dismembered head and hands
scattered across the barren landscape
reinforce this theme
showing that all things
no matter how great
are eventually forgotten or reduced to ruins. Another key theme is the contrast between past and present. The solitary figure standing before the ruins represents the present
observing the remains of the past. The figure's modern attire contrasts sharply with the ancient
decayed statues
highlighting the passage of time and the cyclical nature of history. This suggests that while individuals and societies may believe in their enduring significance
they are ultimately transient
destined to become relics of a forgotten era. The cracked stone head
bearing your likeness
reflects the inevitable deterioration of identity and self. Just as the once-imposing statue has been reduced to fragments
so too is the self subject to the forces of time
erosion
and decay. The serene yet broken expression on the stone face suggests a sense of acceptance or resignation
as though acknowledging the impermanence of all things. The hands
particularly the one giving the middle finger

add a layer of irony and defiance. The gesture of defiance—once a symbol of power or rebellion—has now become meaningless in the face of time's unrelenting march. This reinforces the idea that even the boldest declarations of power or resistance are ultimately rendered insignificant in the grand scheme of things. The desert setting further amplifies these themes of isolation
decay
and timelessness. Deserts are often associated with emptiness and the passage of time
and here they serve as a stage for the slow erosion of human creations and ambitions. The setting sun casts a fading light over the ruins
symbolizing the end of an era and the inevitable decline of all that once seemed permanent.
images.teaser: What remains of us when ambition outlives the body but not time?

images.name: Patryioshka
images.description: In the image titled "Patryoshka," a surreal visual unfolds, reminiscent of the traditional Russian Matryoshka dolls. The protagonist is seen emerging from the broken shell of an egg, but what makes this image distinct is the infinite recursive nature of the scene. Within the egg, the protagonist can be seen holding another, smaller egg containing a miniature version of himself. This pattern repeats multiple times, each time with the protagonist in the same position, seemingly guarding and observing the next version of himself as it hatches.
themes: The central theme of "Patryoshka" is infinite recursion and self-reflection. The protagonist’s presence within multiple layers of eggs
each containing a smaller version of himself
symbolizes the concept of endless introspection. Like the Russian Matryoshka doll
where each layer reveals another within
this image reflects the layers of the self
where deeper understanding or realization might reveal more questions or further layers to explore. The act of hatching represents not just a physical birth
but also a metaphorical rebirth
suggesting that self-exploration and personal growth are continuous processes. Another theme is the fragility of life and identity. The eggshell
a symbol of new beginnings and protection
is cracked open
revealing the protagonist inside. However
the cracks also represent vulnerability

reminding us that self-discovery often requires breaking through the fragile layers that we construct around ourselves. The repeated hatching reinforces the idea that this process happens continuously throughout one’s life. The hands around the egg may symbolize external forces—whether societal
familial
or internal—that nurture
protect

or manipulate this process of self-formation. The theme of cyclical time is also explored in the recursive imagery. The endless repetition of the protagonist hatching from within himself suggests that time is not linear but rather cyclical. Each version of the protagonist emerges as a new iteration
yet he is bound within the same cycle. This challenges the viewer to consider how much of life is repeated
and how our past selves influence the present and future versions of us. Finally

the theme of control and autonomy is subtly present. The large hands encircling the eggshell hint at an external force or consciousness overseeing the protagonist’s emergence. While the protagonist actively holds the smaller version of himself

it raises the question of how much control we truly have over our own development. The image suggests a tension between autonomy and the influence of larger forces that shape who we are and who we become.
images.teaser: How many selves can we contain before identity becomes an endless recursion?

images.name: Peeking
images.description: Thematically, the image explores the interplay between identity and illusion, positing the reflection as a sentient force striving to break free of its confines. This perspective invites questions about the nature of self-awareness: whether one’s reflection is merely a passive duplication or a hidden dimension of the psyche. The vivid green setting imparts a dreamlike quality, suggesting that the mirror functions as a portal between the tangible world and a deeper, possibly subconscious realm. By depicting the reflection’s apparent struggle for autonomy, the scene prompts reflection—both literal and metaphorical—on how we perceive ourselves and the extent to which our inner selves remain unseen or restrained by the boundaries of everyday reality.
themes: In this image
a bald
older man wearing a blue sweater and matching jeans stands in a richly hued
green-paneled room
positioned before a large
elaborately carved mirror whose frame echoes the room’s emerald tone. He leans in toward the reflective surface
and the partial visibility of his mirrored counterpart suggests an uncanny
independent presence reaching out beyond the glass. The ornate scrollwork of the mirror contrasts with the clean lines of the surrounding walls
heightening the overall surreal atmosphere. The unified color palette underscores the tension between the man and his reflection
as though the reflection itself is attempting to cross into the physical space.
images.teaser: What urges us to watch life indirectly rather than step fully into it?

images.name: Reflections at the Museum
images.description: In the artwork titled "Reflections in the Museum," the protagonist is seen from behind, standing in front of a large framed painting. The figure appears to be contemplating the artwork, but the scene becomes more complex as the reflection of this figure in the painting seems to repeat endlessly within the frame. The protagonist, dressed in a black and gray jacket with his back to the viewer, stands quietly, fully immersed in the act of observing the painting.
themes: One prominent theme in "Reflections in the Museum" is the exploration of self-reflection and identity. The endless reflections of the protagonist in the painting suggest a deeper meditation on the self
as if the act of observing the artwork becomes a mirror for self-examination. The museum setting
often associated with history and preservation
adds to this theme of introspection
as the figure contemplates not just the painting
but his place in relation to it
perhaps reflecting on personal experiences or memories. Another key theme is the juxtaposition of reality and imagination. The surreal painting within the frame
with its whimsical
fantasy-like imagery
contrasts sharply with the sober
realistic depiction of the museum and the figure. This contrast suggests a dialogue between the real and the imagined
the tangible and the abstract

creating a tension between what is seen and what is felt or experienced internally. The idea of infinite reflection also points to the theme of time and the passage of life. The endless repetition of the protagonist within the framed image may symbolize the cycles of thought
memory
or experience
suggesting that self-reflection is a continuous process
influenced by both the past and the present. The protagonist’s quiet stance hints at a contemplative state
where the boundaries between observer and the observed blur. The painting itself
with its playful depiction of the woman with butterfly wings
introduces the idea of transformation and freedom. Butterflies often symbolize change and liberation
perhaps suggesting that the protagonist is reflecting on his own transformation or journey through life. The museum setting provides a grounding context
while the surreal painting invites the viewer into a world of imagination
suggesting that self-reflection involves navigating both the real and the imagined.
images.teaser: What do we seek in art when reflection turns the viewer inward?

images.name: Room 101
images.description: In the image titled "Room 101," the protagonist crouches in the corner of a dark, dilapidated room, echoing the claustrophobic atmosphere described in George Orwell’s 1984. The room is bare, with peeling walls covered in graffiti and patches of decay, evoking a sense of neglect and despair. The protagonist, dressed in dark clothing, appears small and vulnerable, his hands covering his ears as though to block out some unbearable noise or experience. He is positioned in the far corner of the room, isolated and seemingly consumed by fear or anguish.
